headline: SpaceX Debuts Strong; Fed Eyes Trimmed Mean PCE; US Curbs Anthropic AI Exports
summary: SpaceX IPO saw strong demand, briefly becoming the 4th largest US-listed company by market cap, though broader tech indices declined. The Fed's new Chair, Kevin Warsh, is set to debut at FOMC, with focus on Trimmed Mean PCE and potential rate cut arguments. US imposed export controls on Anthropic's top AI models, citing national security.

bullets.lead: Google diversifies TPU production amid TSMC capacity constraints.

segments.text: Google is reportedly considering Samsung for partial production of its 10th-gen TPU 'Icefish', leveraging Samsung's 2nm process for memory I/O dies, while TSMC handles the 1.4nm core engine, driven by TSMC's capacity constraints from Nvidia AI chip demand.
This move could significantly strengthen Samsung's AI chip foundry references, following Tesla AI6 and Nvidia's next-gen LPU.

sources.title: FPGA firm Efinix lines up TSMC Japan fab as edge AI business grows

bullets.lead: Qualcomm is in talks to acquire Tenstorrent.

segments.text: Qualcomm is reportedly negotiating to acquire Tenstorrent, a RISC-V startup founded by Jim Keller, to expand its AI chip capabilities, potentially offering an alternative to Intel and ARM architectures.

bullets.lead: China's AI chip self-sufficiency faces significant hurdles.

segments.text: China's mandate for 80% domestic AI chip procurement effectively limits Nvidia and AMD usage, making its AI infrastructure expansion dependent on SMIC's 7nm N+2 process, which already operates at over 93% utilization.
Furthermore, a shortage of HBM supply within China could constrain Huawei Ascend accelerator production, with Chinese firms potentially unable to meet domestic AI chip demand by 2030.

bullets.lead: AI optical interconnects and advanced packaging gain traction.

segments.text: The market is seeing significant advancements and demand in optical transceivers, including 1.6T, Co-Packaged Optics (CPO), and Electro-optic Light Source (ELSFP) technologies, while advanced packaging, including glass substrates and Through-Glass Via (TGV) technology, is becoming critical for overcoming bandwidth bottlenecks and yield issues in high-performance computing.

bullets.lead: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh is set to debut amid rate cut pressure.

segments.text: Kevin Warsh will chair his first FOMC meeting on June 18, facing pressure from President Trump for rate cuts and market concerns about a potential "Fed taming" period post-inauguration.

sources.title: Opinion | Kevin Warsh Returns the Fed to a More Disciplined Model
Warsh Fed Era Heralds a New Trial for Asian Currencies
Warsh’s push to axe Fed guidance may lift US borrowing costs, investors warn
'Regime change but in a velvet glove': How Kevin Warsh has set out to remake the Fed

segments.text: Warsh is expected to emphasize the Trimmed Mean PCE, which excludes volatile price movements, as a basis for advocating rate reductions, despite the broader impact of oil prices on overall inflation.

sources.title: Bond Traders Burned by Fed’s Pivot Look to Prices Gauge, Oil

segments.text: The Bank of Japan is expected to raise its benchmark interest rate from 0.75% to 1.0% today, marking the first time it has reached 1% since 1995, with economists anticipating further increases to 1.25% by year-end.

bullets.lead: Oil prices drop on Hormuz reopening hopes.

segments.text: WTI crude prices fell to $81 following news of a US-Iran Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) signing on June 19, raising expectations for the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and boosting global financial markets.

sources.title: Stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq, Dow futures slide with US-Iran peace and inflation in focus
Bond Traders Burned by Fed’s Pivot Look to Prices Gauge, Oil
Brent oil rises more than $1/bbl after bumpy start to US-Iran peace talks
Oil Rises Amid Uncertainty Over Strait of Hormuz

segments.text: Despite May Producer Price Index (PPI) exceeding expectations due to energy costs, core PPI came in lower, mitigating concerns about widespread inflationary pressures.

bullets.lead: US imposes AI export controls on Anthropic models.

segments.text: The US government has implemented export controls on Anthropic's top-tier AI models, Mythos 5 and Fable 5, restricting foreign access due to national security concerns, marking the first instance of government intervention on AI models.

sources.title: Exclusive: Trump tells "The Axios Show" that Anthropic was a national security threat

segments.text: This action follows claims that a company was able to "jailbreak" the Mythos model's safeguards, prompting the Commerce Secretary to issue a letter to Anthropic.

bullets.lead: China accelerates NEV heavy truck adoption.

segments.text: China's Ministry of Transport aims to increase NEV heavy truck penetration to 40% by 2030, up from 29% in 2025, targeting over 1.6 million units in operation.
The plan includes establishing 3,000 charging/battery swap stations and a 30,000 km green freight network, supported by financial incentives and new business models like battery leasing.

bullets.lead: US-Iran peace talks progress, causing oil price volatility.

segments.text: President Trump indicated that an agreement with Iran is in its final stages, with a signing ceremony possibly occurring in Europe this weekend, leading to initial drops in oil prices.

sources.title: Oil Climbs After Fresh Trump Threat as US-Iran Peace Talks Begin
Sizing Up Trump’s Iran Deal
Trump hails Iran deal but conflict continues to cast long shadow over global economy

segments.text: However, oil prices later climbed amid renewed threats from Trump and uncertainty over the Strait of Hormuz, with US strategic oil reserves nearing a 43-year low.

sources.title: Oil Climbs After Fresh Trump Threat as US-Iran Peace Talks Begin
Brent oil rises more than $1/bbl after bumpy start to US-Iran peace talks
Oil Rises Amid Uncertainty Over Strait of Hormuz

bullets.lead: SpaceX IPO debuts strong, but lock-up concerns emerge.

segments.text: SpaceX successfully debuted at $160.1, becoming the 6th largest US-listed company by market cap, with expectations for index inclusion (Russell 1000, MSCI, NASDAQ100) by June-July driving passive fund inflows.

sources.title: Musk's SpaceX overtakes Amazon to become world's fifth most valuable firm
Stocks making the biggest moves premarket: Intel, SpaceX, Micron, Carnival & more

segments.text: However, the company's lock-up period is shorter than industry standards, with significant portions of protected shares becoming eligible for sale after Q2 earnings, and further unlocks at 70, 90, 105, 120, and 135 days post-IPO, concentrating a large volume of potential sales in Q3.

bullets.lead: OpenAI and Anthropic face price war pressures.

segments.text: OpenAI is reportedly considering substantial token price reductions to compete with Anthropic for enterprise clients, a move that could further strain profitability for both companies given their already significant compute costs and billions in losses.

sources.title: Accenture Stock Falls 18% as Lower Revenue Projection Feeds AI Fears

segments.text: The impending price competition will test the business models of both OpenAI and Anthropic as they approach potential IPOs, with investors viewing the substitutability of their products as a fundamental risk.

bullets.lead: The memory market enters a supercycle.

segments.text: The memory market is experiencing a supercycle with significant year-over-year price increases for DRAM, Flash, and HBM, driven by strong AI demand and undersupply, with hyperscalers being key drivers.
Taiwan memory chip designers are poised for a 245% revenue jump due to surging AI storage demand.

sources.title: Taiwan memory chip designers set for 245% revenue jump on AI storage demand

bullets.lead: SK Hynix discusses US HBM supply and investment.

segments.text: SK Hynix reportedly held discussions in the US regarding HBM supply and potential local investment plans, as the company navigates the high demand for advanced memory solutions.

sources.title: SK Hynix reportedly held U.S. talks on HBM supply and local investment plans

bullets.lead: OpenAI pivots to a superapp strategy.

segments.text: OpenAI is preparing its largest overhaul since ChatGPT's launch, aiming to transform the chatbot into a "superapp" that integrates coding tools, AI agents, image generation, and external applications to boost profitability and shift focus from simple Q&A to high-value enterprise products.
This strategic shift reflects a belief that the future of AI lies in agents performing complex tasks, aligning with Anthropic's focus on enterprise AI.

sources.title: EXCLUSIVE: Google And Meta May Have The Most To Lose In The AI‑Agent Era, Says Founder Who Rejected OpenAI
Google DeepMind maps four paths from AGI to ASI

bullets.lead: Amazon drops Sam Altman movie amid OpenAI partnership.

segments.text: Amazon MGM Studio has reportedly dropped a movie about OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, a decision that comes after Amazon announced a partnership with OpenAI, raising questions about potential conflicts of interest or strategic alignment.

sources.title: Amazon’s Movie Arm Abandons Film About OpenAI
Amazon MGM Studio Drops Altman Movie
Luca Guadagnino’s Sam Altman movie dropped by Amazon after it announces OpenAI partnership

x_themes.summary: The market is seeing significant advancements and demand in optical transceivers, including 1.6T, Co-Packaged Optics (CPO), and Electro-optic Light Source (ELSFP) technologies, driven by AI data center needs. Companies like Sivers, OE Solutions, Marvell, and Jabil are key players in developing and supplying these next-gen optical architectures, with some concerns about potential oversupply being dismissed.

The memory market is experiencing a supercycle with significant year-over-year price increases for DRAM, Flash, and HBM, driven by strong AI demand and undersupply. Hyperscalers are projected to dramatically increase their capex allocation to memory, leading to potential upside surprises for companies like Micron and Kioxia, whose valuations are still considered low.

Advanced packaging, including glass substrates and Through-Glass Via (TGV) technology, is becoming critical for overcoming bandwidth bottlenecks and yield issues in high-performance AI chips by enabling tighter integration of smaller chiplets. Companies like LPKF and JNTC are developing key equipment and processes for glass-based advanced packaging, with major semiconductor clients and foundries like TSMC and Intel exploring these solutions.

The valuation of AI Large Language Model (LLM) companies, particularly Chinese frontier labs like Zhipu (Knowledge Atlas) and Minimax, is drawing attention, with some reaching high ARR figures. There are discussions around the competitive landscape with US players like Anthropic and OpenAI, and regulatory scrutiny on AI models and prediction markets.

The semiconductor industry is facing various supply chain bottlenecks and geopolitical pressures, including a shortage of E-glass fabric for PCBs (impacting Nvidia suppliers), a halt in tungsten material flow for PCB tools, and Taiwan's surging electricity demand driven by AI data centers and semiconductor manufacturing. US concerns about China's access to top chip tools also highlight geopolitical tensions in the supply chain.

The concept of AI compute becoming a commodity is emerging, with CME and ICE considering listing compute as a futures commodity, shifting focus to production bottlenecks in specialized tools and testing. Nvidia's Vera Rubin-based AI data centers are highlighted for their massive cost and power demands, driving the evolution of data center architecture and the need for efficient cooling solutions like microfluidics.

The space sector is seeing significant activity, including the anticipated float unlock timeline for SpaceX's IPO, and trading opportunities in companies like AST SpaceMobile ($ASTS) and Rocket Lab ($RKLB) based on batch deployment success and sector-specific influences.

GLP-1 receptor agonists and dual GIP/GLP-1 receptor agonists ($LLY, $NVO) are being explored for their potential to reduce impulsivity and addictive behaviors, including gambling and compulsive trading. While direct clinical evidence for gambling disorder is early, mechanistic neuroscience and alcohol-use-disorder trials suggest these drugs could make investors more measured by dampening cue-triggered urges and loss-chasing.
